figures.caption: Figure 3: Distributions of configuration-level expected total explanation scores 𝐸 [∑𝐶𝑖 ] in isolated, two-way, and three-way batches. Open circles mark the BASELINE score for each batch. Boxes show the IQR; center lines show the median; dots show the mean; whiskers show min/max.

Table 1: AQ1: Agreement between individual human labels and LLM-as-a-judge labels for C2–C4 and C6 (𝑛 = 288 labels per criterion and model). Model columns report accuracy (Acc); “LLM (avg.)” is the macro-average across the three models.

Table 2: AQ2: Pass prevalence for individual human and LLM-as-a-judge labels for C2–C4 and C6 (𝑛 = 288 labels per criterion and model). The human column reports the human pass share, model columns report LLM pass shares, and “LLM (avg.)” is the macro-average across the three models.

Figure 2: Distributions of expected total explanation scores 𝐸 [∑𝐶𝑖 ] across defects for each model’s top-3 isolated context modules (ranked by mean 𝐸 [∑𝐶𝑖 ]) and BASELINE. Boxes show the IQR; center lines show the median; dots show the mean; whiskers show min/max. Abbreviations: ERR=ERROR, DOC=DOCSTRING, BLK=SLICE_BLOCK, BWD=SLICE_BACKWARD, FWD=SLICE_FORWARD, UNI=SLICE_UNION, BASE=BASELINE.

Figure 5: Effect sizes comparing high- vs. low-quality explanations on passing fixes in composed batches (Δ =Q4–Q1). Blue filled circles denote two-way batches; orange open circles denote three-way batches. Whiskers show Bonferroni-adjusted defect-bootstrap CIs (𝑚 = 2 per model). Negative values for line deviation, normalized Levenshtein, and spurious changed lines indicate more reference-like fixes; positive ΔΔVol. indicates a larger complexity increase (Fix − Baseline).

Figure 1: Study overview: from 12 Defects4J defects translated from Java to Python (contamination control: Java NO_EXPLANATION baseline; manual inspection), we vary context composition (8 modules; 93 configurations), generate failure explanations (3 models × 3 runs; temperature = 0 where possible), score them via deterministic checks and LLM-as-a-judge, analyze criterion discriminativeness (RQ2) and context sensitivity (RQ3), validate judge ratings via a separate human user study (RQ1), and assess downstream utility via fix generation and fix validation with and without explanations (RQ4).
